mechanism_narrative: A persistent multi-month rise in the effective federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as tightening expectations build.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20-day returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following rate increases are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a full-employment threshold and stays there, markets anticipate accelerating wage inflation and more aggressive Fed tightening, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following the UNRATE<4.0% trigger are positive or statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline returns (p > 0.05) across at least two historical episodes visible pre-2017-12-15 in the training sample used for calibration.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and retail sales signals broad-based, self-reinforcing economic expansion across both supply and demand sides of the economy, which is not captured by either series alone and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in INDPRO and RSAFS fails to outperform either single-series signal in out-of-sample rank IC, or produces forward returns statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts occurring during a late-cycle expansion with rising policy rates signals an early rollover in interest-rate-sensitive demand that historically precedes broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, but this signal is weak or absent early in expansions when rates are low.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with rising interest rates
failure_condition: HOUST declines of >5% over 3 months during late-cycle regimes are followed by non-negative median 60-day forward returns, or the effect disappears when conditioned on regime (i.e., same signal in early-cycle low-rate periods produces equally negative returns, disproving regime dependence).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in headline CPI above a threshold rate raises near-term expectations of faster Fed tightening, causing a short-lived risk-off repricing in equities and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
regime_hint: rising inflation with active Fed tightening
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following CPI YoY breaches above 2.5% show no significant negative skew relative to baseline (p > 0.05), or hit rate of negative returns is below 55% across triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment reflects continued labor market strength and consumer income growth, supporting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month momentum and forward 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive payroll growth precedes negative returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in real GDP growth above trend signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate revenue prospects, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 growth prints above 2.5% annualized show no statistically significant improvement over baseline (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one of the two most recent comparable episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed is actively raising rates while unemployment sits at cyclical lows, the joint configuration signals imminent restrictive monetary policy targeting an overheating labor market, producing a short-horizon negative equity reaction as markets price faster tightening than either series alone would imply.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for 3 consecutive months AND UNRATE < 4.2%
regime_hint: late-cycle low unemployment with active Fed tightening
failure_condition: Joint FEDFUNDS-rising and UNRATE-low trigger produces 5-day forward returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction signal underperforms either univariate signal alone in out-of-sample rank IC.
